name: Bagels (Breadmaker)
recipeInstructions:
1. Set the machine to dough cycle.
2. Let the machine knead the dough once, then let it rise for only 20 minutes in the machine. If your cycle runs longer, remove the dough after 20 minutes and turn off the machine.
3. Divide the dough into the appropriate number of pieces (8, 12, or 16 oz).
4. Roll each piece into a rope and form it into a circle, pressing the ends together. You may need to wet one end slightly to help seal the ends.
5. Place the bagels on a well-greased baking sheet, cover, and let rise for an additional 15 to 20 minutes.
6. In a nonaluminum pan (such as a cast iron frying pan), bring water to a slight boil.
7. Carefully lower about 3 or 4 bagels at a time into the boiling water and cook for about 30 seconds on each side.
8. Remove the bagels with a slotted spoon, drain on a towel, and sprinkle with poppy seeds, sesame seeds, or dried onion bits if desired.
9. Place the drained bagels back on the greased baking sheet.
10. Bake in a preheated oven at 550°F (or 290°C) for 8 minutes.
